title: Unite Demonstration VM (2022)
author: Ondřej Vašíček
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This VM is meant as a demonstration of Unite---an adapter for transforming analysis tools to web services via OSLC. Usage instructions are in a readme file located on the desktop of the VM or in "/home/unite/unite-artifact/". The login password is the same as the username ("unite"). 

 The VM contains Unite configured with a number of analysis tools to demonstrate how to use Unite and that all of the analysis tools can be executed through it. The VM features two videos one of which covers the whole demonstration process along with a detailed readme which explains all demo examples in detail. All the demonstration steps are performed using Postman, a REST client which we used to interact with Unite's REST API. The artifact also demonstrates how to create configuration for a new analysis tool to be able to use it through Unite. 

 A few steps in the artifact demonstration require internet access for the VM. These steps are optional and can be skipped if you do not want to enable internet connection. The steps include executing analysis using ANaConDA because it is only available remotely due to not working on Ubuntu 20, and two ways of transferring SUT source files (download from a URL, and cloning a Git repository). 

   

 Links & Acknowledgements 

 
	 Unite Git repository - https://pajda.fit.vutbr.cz/verifit/unite 
	 Postman - https://www.postman.com/product/what-is-postman/ 
	 Postman Terms of Service - https://www.postman.com/legal/terms/ 
	 Licenses of included analysis tools can be found in the VM in "/home/unite/unite-artifact/licenses" 
	 The VM image is based on TACAS22 AE VM (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5562597)
